mechanism_narrative: A stronger 3-month trend in retail sales raises near-term revenue expectations and risk appetite, so higher RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher average 20-day forward S&P 500 returns than lower RSAFS growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and higher recession risk, causing lower earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases and the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment is rising and unemployment is falling, labor income growth supports consumption without signaling labor-market stress, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not outperform the unconditional 20-day S&P 500 return by at least 25 basis points on average in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing into a higher federal funds rate regime raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions, so increases in FEDFUNDS predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its prior 12-month maximum by at least 0.25 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS upside crossings are followed by nonnegative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A positive industrial production trend signals stronger real output and operating leverage, increasing expected corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 6-month INDPRO growth is not associated with higher 60-day S&P 500 forward returns, or if the top-minus-bottom tercile spread is less than or equal to zero in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in housing starts signals weakening household formation, construction demand, and durable-goods spillovers, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 6-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside crossings are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains pinned, markets price future policy tightening and margin pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth rises above its 12-month average while FEDFUNDS changes less than 0.10 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-acceleration and stable-FEDFUNDS interaction is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or has a nonnegative average triggered return.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with a low federal funds rate raises expected cash flows while keeping discount rates low, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth is positive and FEDFUNDS is below 1.00%
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth with FEDFUNDS below 1.00% does not deliver higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than periods with weak GDPC1 growth or higher FEDFUNDS.
